title: Shallow Water
description: **Shallow Water Equations.** Depth-averaged free-surface flow with bed topography and optional Manning friction (`n_m`); the level-0 base model.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import SWE
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Shallow water equations (+ bed, optional Manning friction via n_m).
model = SystemModel.from_model(SWE(dimension=1, parameters={"g": 9.81, "n_m": 0.0}))

title: Shallow Moments
description: **Shallow Moment Equations (SME).** Depth-averaged flow resolving a polynomial vertical velocity profile through `level` moments (Kowalski & Torrilhon); `level = 0` recovers shallow water.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import SME
from zoomy_core.model.models.closures import Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Shallow Moment Equations: `level` velocity-profile moments (level 0 = SWE).
# closures close tau_xz: Newtonian bulk + Navier-slip bed + stress-free surface.
model = SystemModel.from_model(SME(
    level=2,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree()],
))

title: Multi-layer Shallow Water
description: **Multi-layer Shallow Water.** `n_layers` surface-following layers coupled by an interface mass-flux closure; resolves vertical structure without going fully 3-D.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import MLSWE
from zoomy_core.model.models.closures import UpwindInterface
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Multi-layer shallow water; UpwindInterface closes the interface transfer velocity.
model = SystemModel.from_model(MLSWE(
    n_layers=2,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[UpwindInterface()],
))

title: Multi-layer Shallow Moments
description: Layered shallow moments: n_layers x (level+1) velocity moments per column.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import MLSME
from zoomy_core.model.models.closures import Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree, UpwindInterface
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Multi-layer shallow moments; UpwindInterface closes the interface transfer,
# Newtonian bulk + Navier-slip bed + stress-free surface close tau_xz.
model = SystemModel.from_model(MLSME(
    n_layers=2,
    level=1,
    dimension=2,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree(), UpwindInterface()],
))

title: VAM
description: **Vertically-Averaged Moment model (VAM).** Non-hydrostatic moment closure (`level` = truncation order) for dispersive shallow flow; u, w and the non-hydrostatic pressure share the basis.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import VAM
from zoomy_core.model.models.closures import Newtonian, NavierSlip, StressFree
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Non-hydrostatic vertically-averaged moment model (`level` = truncation order).
# closures close tau_xz: Newtonian bulk + Navier-slip bed + stress-free surface.
model = SystemModel.from_model(VAM(
    level=1,
    dimension=2,
    small_slope=True,
    closures=[Newtonian(), NavierSlip(), StressFree()],
))

title: 3-D sigma-coordinate (Sigma3D)
description: Sigma-coordinate 3-D model; runs via its dedicated split solver (Sigma3DSplitSolver), not the generic backends.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models.sigma3d import Sigma3D
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# 3-D sigma-coordinate model (conservative-sigma form).
model = SystemModel.from_model(Sigma3D())

title: Tutorial: SWE Dam Break
description:
**Tutorial**: 1D dam-break with SWE.

A classic test case: water at h=2 on the left, h=1 on the right.
The dam breaks and a shock + rarefaction wave develop.
template:
from zoomy_core.model.models import SWE
import zoomy_core.model.boundary_conditions as BC
import zoomy_core.model.initial_conditions as IC
import numpy as np
from zoomy_core.systemmodel.system_model import SystemModel

# Dam break: still water h=2 (left) / h=1 (right), flat bed, open ends.
def dam_break_ic(x):
    return np.array([0.0, 2.0 if x[0] < 5.0 else 1.0, 0.0])

model = SystemModel.from_model(SWE(
    dimension=1,
    initial_conditions=IC.UserFunction(function=dam_break_ic),
    boundary_conditions=BC.BoundaryConditions([
        BC.Extrapolation(tag="left"),
        BC.Extrapolation(tag="right"),
    ]),
))
